# MetaPeek

> Inspect, preview, and fix HTML meta tags and Open Graph markup.

MetaPeek is a free, open-source tool by the Illinois Criminal Justice Information Authority (ICJIA) that analyzes how web pages appear when shared on social media platforms.

## What MetaPeek Does

- Previews link appearance on 7 platforms: Google, Facebook, LinkedIn, X (Twitter), WhatsApp, Slack, iMessage
- Scores meta tag quality 0–100 with letter grades (A–F) across 7 weighted categories
- Detects missing, duplicate, or misconfigured Open Graph, Twitter Card, and standard meta tags
- Validates OG image dimensions against platform-specific requirements
- Assesses AI readiness: structured data, authorship, canonical URLs, robots.txt, llms.txt
- Provides 10 advisory SEO insight checks: charset, favicon, viewport, og:type, image alt text, hreflang, H1 tags, resource hints
- Generates copy-paste HTML fixes and exports reports as JSON, Markdown, or HTML

## Modes

- **Paste HTML** — Paste a page's head section for instant offline analysis
- **Fetch URL** — Enter any URL to analyze a live page via server-side proxy

## API

GET /api/analyze?url=<encoded-url> — Returns JSON with parsed meta tags, diagnostics, quality score, and timing.

## Links

- Live app: https://metapeek.icjia.app
- Source code: https://github.com/ICJIA/icjia-metapeek
- ICJIA: https://icjia.illinois.gov
